item.title: Malachi Wrampling's debut Blues season ends in suspension
item.description: Malachi Wrampling's debut Blues season ends in suspension

item.title: Jai Narayan College makes history participating in Vodafone Deans this year
item.description: Jai Narayan College is making history this year as it prepares for its first-ever appearance in the Fiji Secondary Schools Vodafone Deans competition. This was shared by Head Coach for the Under-17 grade Josua Moko to the media during the launching at Gospel.
item.content:encoded:
Jai Narayan College is making history this year as it prepares for its first-ever appearance in the Fiji Secondary Schools Vodafone Deans competition.

This was shared by Head Coach for the Under-17 grade Josua Moko to the media during the launching at Gospel.

Moko says the Deans competition provides a valuable pathway for young athletes across Fiji, creating possibilities not only for boys but also for girls who aspire to pursue rugby at higher levels.

Moko stressed that taking part in the Southern Zone is an investment in helping students grow both on and off the field while exposing them to opportunities that could shape their futures.

He says this prepares the players hollistically.

Moko says it's an ideal time to represent their school and possibly secure a contract overseas and represent their country

item.title: New Zealand's finest battle for supremacy in Super Rugby semi-finals
item.description: The regular season is over, and now the spotlight shines on four New Zealand giants standing tall in the Super Rugby Pacific semifinals. Kini Naholo's Hurricanes arrive with speed, flair, and belief.
item.content:encoded:
The regular season is over, and now the spotlight shines on four New Zealand giants standing tall in the Super Rugby Pacific semifinals.

Kini Naholo's Hurricanes arrive with speed, flair, and belief.

Battle hardened and fearless after a superb quarter final game against the Brumbies they carry the hopes of Wellington and a hunger to turn promise into silverware.

Hoskin Satutu's Blues march forward with power and purpose and the Auckland's warriors know what it takes to win, and they are determined to prove their reign is far from over.

From the south come the defending champions winning the tittle 15 times over the past years Sevu Reece and the Crusaders who now stand just two victories away from adding another chapter to rugby's greatest dynasty.

Emoni Narawa's Chiefs from Waikato are driven by unfinished business and powerful, relentless, and united, they seek the title that has narrowly escaped them and seek redemtion.

The Crusaders will face the Chiefs on Friday at Waikato at 7.05pm and the Hurricanes will play the Blues on Saturday at Wellington at 7.05pm.

item.title: Springboks add Buthelezi and Sadie ahead of international season
item.description: The Springboks have boosted their squad with the inclusion of loose forward Phepsi Buthelezi and prop Carlu Sadie ahead of their international season. Buthelezi, who plays for the Sharks and made his Test debut against Portugal in 2024, will join the squad in Johannesburg, while France-based prop Sadie is expected to arrive on Wednesday.
item.content:encoded:
The Springboks have boosted their squad with the inclusion of loose forward Phepsi Buthelezi and prop Carlu Sadie ahead of their international season.

Buthelezi, who plays for the Sharks and made his Test debut against Portugal in 2024, will join the squad in Johannesburg, while France-based prop Sadie is expected to arrive on Wednesday.

The Springboks have also welcomed back players from the Stormers following their return from Ireland after their United Rugby Championship semifinal.

South Africa began on-field preparations on Monday ahead of their season-opening matches against the Barbarians and Zimbabwe A at Nelson Mandela Bay Stadium in Gqeberha on June 20th.

Springbok coach Rassie Erasmus says there is a real sense of excitement within the squad as they prepare for the new season.

He believes the new additions will settle into the group quickly.

The Springboks are expected to name their squad on June 21st for the opening Nations Championship matches against England, Scotland and Wales.

item.title: RKS U-18 Rugby Skipper confident of a good Deans season
item.description: Ratu Kadavulevu School Under-18 rugby captain Esira Batisoso believes his team is well-prepared for another challenging Vodafone Deans season following valuable international exposure in Japan earlier this year. Batisoso says the experience gained at the Sanix Tournament in Japan had significantly enhanced the team's development.
item.content:encoded:
Ratu Kadavulevu School Under-18 rugby captain Esira Batisoso believes his team is well-prepared for another challenging Vodafone Deans season following valuable international exposure in Japan earlier this year.

Batisoso says the experience gained at the Sanix Tournament in Japan had significantly enhanced the team's development.

The Nalotawa, Yawe, Kadavu player says that competing against high-quality opposition exposed the team to a higher level of pace and intensity, helping them better understand the demands of top-level school rugby.

Despite leading the defending champions, Batisoso acknowledged that retaining the title will be a difficult task.

He says he has confidence in this team, but they know defending the Deans Trophy will not be easy.

Batisoso says they cannot afford to take any team lightly because the competition will be tough throughout the season.

Batisoso is leading RKS in their 2026 Vodafone Deans Trophy title defence campaign after the side opened their season in a dominant 46-3 victory over Sila Central High School.

The young captain remains optimistic they will pursue back-to-back Deans titles.

In the Eastern division games on Saturday, RKS will face Queen Victoria School in their second pool match while Lelean Memorial School will take on Saint John's Cawaci in the other pool match.

Sila Central College is on a bye this week.

item.title: Koroisau makes Blues squad for Origin Game Two
item.description: Vodafone Fiji Bati and Wests Tigers hooker Apisai Koroisau has been named by Westpac NSW Blues coach Laurie Daley in his 21-player

item.title: Netherlands' Timber out of World Cup with injury
item.description: Arsenal defender Jurrien Timber has been ruled out of the Netherlands' World Cup campaign with a groin injury and replaced by Luts

item.title: Labasa boosts their squad with PNG internationals ahead of Fiji FACT
item.description: Labasa has strengthened its squad ahead of the BiC Fiji FACT 2026 by securing the services of Papua New Guinea national representatives Rex Naime and Joseph Joe from Hekari United. 22-year-old Naime featured prominently for Hekari United in the 2025 OFC Champions League, while 23-year-old Joe has earned recognition for his performances in midfield.
item.content:encoded:
Labasa has strengthened its squad ahead of the BiC Fiji FACT 2026 by securing the services of Papua New Guinea national representatives Rex Naime and Joseph Joe from Hekari United.

22-year-old Naime featured prominently for Hekari United in the 2025 OFC Champions League, while 23-year-old Joe has earned recognition for his performances in midfield.

Both players bring experience from regional competitions, including the OFC Champions League and OFC Professional League.

They are expected to provide added depth to the Babasiga Lions squad as Labasa prepares to host the tournament at Subrail Park.

Their inclusion is a welcome boost for Labasa as the team looks to make a strong showing in the competition.

Meanwhile, the BiC Fiji FACT pool matches will be played from 19th to 21st June before the action returns for the semi-finals on 27th June and the grand final on 28th June at Subrail Park.

item.title: Drova to play in his first Fiji FACT for hometown Navua

item.title: Tony Brown to jump ship from Springboks to All Blacks after RWC 2027
item.description: New Zealand Rugby (NZR) has confirmed that Tony Brown will leave his role with South Africa to join the All Blacks coaching setup
